definitions.term: Three key aspects to consider when evaluating sources
definitions.definition: 1\. Authority: the source is authored or published by a recognized expert, institution, or organization. 2. Accuracy: the content is well-researched, fact-checked, and supported by credible evidence or citations. 3. Objectivity: information is presented fairly and without undue bias.
the raw, unprocessed facts and figures, usually in quantitative forms ​(numbers or figures), without inherent meaning until it is processed.

While it may be impossible for a source to achieve absolute objectivity, researchers can still sharpen their critical thinking skills to spot questionable claims and sensationalist language that's intended to mislead.
organized data that provides meaning, often found as summarized market reports, trend analyses, or aggregated industry statistics.
the recognition of patterns, relationships, or implications from processed information.
the strategic conclusions drawn from knowledge that directly inform business decisions.

formulas.name: EBITDA
formulas.formula: Net Income + Interest + Taxes + Depreciation + Amortization

formulas.name: Breakeven Units
formulas.formula: Fixed Costs / (Price - Variable Cost per Unit)

formulas.name: Price Elasticity of Demand
formulas.formula: % Change in Quantity Demanded / % Change in Price

formulas.name: Cross-Price Elasticity
formulas.formula: % Change in Qty of Good A / % Change in Price of Good B

formulas.name: Income Elasticity
formulas.formula: % Change in Quantity Demanded / % Change in Income

formulas.name: Consumer Surplus
formulas.formula: Max Willingness to Pay - Actual Price

formulas.name: Producer Surplus
formulas.formula: Actual Price - Min Willingness to Accept

formulas.name: Comparative Advantage
formulas.formula: Opportunity Cost of Good A < Partner's Opportunity Cost of Good A

formulas.name: Terms of Trade
formulas.formula: Export Price Index / Import Price Index × 100

formulas.name: Profitability Index
formulas.formula: PV of Future Cash Flows / Initial Investment

formulas.name: BCG Growth-Share
formulas.formula: Relative Market Share vs Market Growth Rate

formulas.name: Customer Acquisition Cost
formulas.formula: Total Marketing Spend / New Customers Acquired

formulas.name: Customer Lifetime Value
formulas.formula: Avg Revenue per Customer × Avg Customer Lifespan

formulas.name: CLV/CAC Ratio
formulas.formula: Customer Lifetime Value / Customer Acquisition Cost

formulas.name: Price Elasticity (Marketing)
formulas.formula: % Change in Demand / % Change in Price

formulas.name: Reorder Point
formulas.formula: Average Daily Demand × Lead Time + Safety Stock

formulas.name: Little's Law
formulas.formula: L = λ × W (Inventory = Throughput × Flow Time)

cases.title: Opening Day
cases.content: Sarah opens a consulting firm: $50K personal investment + $30K bank loan. Buys $20K equipment, keeps $60K cash. Assets $80K = Liabilities $30K + Equity $50K. When she earns $10K: Cash up $10K (debit), Revenue up $10K (credit via equity). New: $90K = $30K + $60K.

cases.title: Accrual vs. Cash
cases.content: Law firm completes $50K case in December, client pays January. Accrual: December revenue. Cash: January revenue. The December income statement, tax liability, and bonuses all differ. Most businesses use accrual to match revenue with expenses incurred to earn it.

cases.title: The Coffee Shop Decision
cases.content: Maria's café: Fixed costs $8K/month, coffee $2 variable, sells at $5. Breakeven = $8,000 / ($5-$2) = 2,667 cups. She sells 3,000 cups. Profit = (3,000 × $3) - $8,000 = $1,000. Adding pastries at $4 (variable $1.50): new weighted CM changes breakeven.

cases.title: Market Disruption
cases.content: When ride-sharing entered the taxi market, supply of transportation increased (shift right) while demand for traditional taxis decreased (shift left). The equilibrium price for taxi rides fell, and many taxi medallion values collapsed from $1M+ to under $200K.

cases.title: The Fed's Dilemma
cases.content: In 2022-2023, the Fed raised rates from near-zero to 5.25-5.50% to combat inflation. This increased borrowing costs, slowed housing, but also raised insurance general account returns, improving IUL cap rates from ~9% to ~11%.

cases.title: Capital Allocation
cases.content: A firm has $10M to invest. Project A: NPV $2.1M, IRR 18%. Project B: NPV $1.8M, IRR 22%. If projects are mutually exclusive, choose A (higher NPV). If independent and capital allows, do both. IRR can mislead with non-conventional cash flows.

cases.title: Premium Financing
cases.content: Client needs $5M death benefit. Annual premium $85K. Loan at SOFR+2.5%. Policy credits 5.5%. Year 1-8: loan balance exceeds cash value (risk zone). Year 9-10: crossover point. Year 15+: cash value significantly exceeds loan. At death: $5M - loan payoff = net to family.

cases.title: Remote Team Challenge
cases.content: A global team spans 4 time zones with members from US, India, Germany, and Brazil. The US manager uses direct communication style, but Indian team members prefer indirect feedback. Solution: establish communication norms, use Hofstede's dimensions to bridge gaps.

cases.title: Strategic Pivot
cases.content: Netflix transitioned from DVD-by-mail to streaming to content production. Each pivot required different capabilities (logistics → technology → creative). Porter's value chain analysis shows how activities shifted. Blue Ocean strategy: created uncontested market space.

cases.title: A/B Testing Campaign
cases.content: An insurance agency tests two email subject lines. Version A: 'Protect Your Family's Future' (emotional). Version B: 'Save 23% on Life Insurance' (rational). A gets 18% open rate, B gets 24%. But A converts at 8% vs B's 3%. CLV of A leads is 2.3x higher.

cases.title: Process Bottleneck
cases.content: Insurance case processing: Application (1 day) → Underwriting (14 days) → Approval (1 day) → Delivery (2 days). Underwriting is the bottleneck. Adding a second underwriter or implementing accelerated underwriting for healthy applicants reduces total cycle from 18 to 7 days.

cases.title: Hiring Decision with Data
cases.content: A manager must choose between two candidates. Regression analysis of past hires shows: years of experience (β=0.3), cultural fit score (β=0.5), and technical test (β=0.2) predict first-year performance. Candidate A: more experience. Candidate B: higher cultural fit. Model favors B.

fs_applications.title: Carrier Financial Analysis
fs_applications.content: Use ratio analysis to evaluate insurance carrier strength. Key metrics: AM Best rating (NLG = A+), RBC ratio (>300% strong), combined ratio (<100% = underwriting profit), investment yield. Compare statutory accounting (SAP) vs GAAP: SAP is more conservative, treats premiums as liabilities until earned.

fs_applications.title: Commission Accounting
fs_applications.content: First-year commissions (FYC) typically 80-110% of target premium for life insurance. Renewal commissions 2-5% years 2-10. Override commissions for managers 5-15% of downline FYC. Revenue recognition: commissions earned when policy is issued and paid, subject to chargeback if policy lapses within first year.

fs_applications.title: Tax Treatment (IRC 7702)
fs_applications.content: Life insurance tax advantages: death benefit income-tax-free (IRC 101), cash value grows tax-deferred, policy loans not taxable if policy stays in force. IRC 7702 defines what qualifies as life insurance. MEC rules (IRC 7702A): if overfunded, loses loan tax advantage. 1035 exchanges allow tax-free replacement.

fs_applications.title: Interest Rate Chain
fs_applications.content: Fed Funds Rate → Treasury Yields → Insurance General Account Returns → IUL Cap Rates & FIA Credits → Client Product Attractiveness. When Fed raises rates, general accounts earn more, carriers can offer higher caps. Current environment (SOFR 3.65%): favorable for accumulation products.

fs_applications.title: Arizona Demographics
fs_applications.content: Arizona population 7.4M, growing 1.5%/year. Median age 38. High retiree migration = estate planning demand. Maricopa County = 62% of state population. Key industries: tech, healthcare, real estate. No state income tax on Social Security. Estate tax: federal only ($13.61M exemption 2024).

fs_applications.title: Premium Financing Modeling
fs_applications.content: Structure: bank lends premium payments, policy is collateral. Key variables: death benefit, annual premium, loan rate (SOFR + spread), policy crediting rate. Crossover point: when cash value exceeds cumulative loan balance. Risk: if crediting rate drops below loan rate, shortfall grows. Stress test at guaranteed minimum rates.

fs_applications.title: Agent Recruiting ROI
fs_applications.content: Cost to recruit and train one agent: $15-25K (licensing, training, marketing, salary draw). Expected first-year production: $50-80K FYC. Override income: 10% of FYC = $5-8K/year. Breakeven: 2-3 years. NPV of a successful agent over 10 years at 8% discount: $45-65K. But only 30-40% reach meaningful production.

fs_applications.title: Agency Culture Building
fs_applications.content: Herzberg's two-factor theory applied: Hygiene factors (competitive contracts, office space, technology) prevent dissatisfaction. Motivators (recognition, advancement, autonomy) drive engagement. Top agencies combine both: strong infrastructure + meaningful recognition programs + clear career paths from agent to manager to director.

fs_applications.title: Distribution Strategy
fs_applications.content: Porter's Five Forces for insurance distribution: High rivalry (thousands of agents), moderate new entrant threat (low barriers), high substitute threat (direct-to-consumer, robo-advisors), moderate supplier power (carriers), high buyer power (price-sensitive consumers). Differentiation strategy: holistic planning, not product pushing.

fs_applications.title: Client Segmentation
fs_applications.content: Use cluster analysis to segment client base: High-Net-Worth (>$1M investable), Mass Affluent ($250K-$1M), Emerging ($100-250K). Each segment has different product needs, service expectations, and profitability. Regression: predict which prospects convert based on age, income, family status, and referral source.

fs_applications.title: Compliance & Marketing
fs_applications.content: FINRA 2210: all communications must be fair, balanced, not misleading. SEC Marketing Rule: performance advertising must include specific disclosures. State insurance advertising rules vary. Arizona ADIFI: all advertising must be filed. Social media: each post is an advertisement. Testimonials now allowed with disclosures.

fs_applications.title: Case Workflow Optimization
fs_applications.content: Insurance case lifecycle: Discovery (1-2 meetings) → Fact Finding → Case Design → Application → Underwriting → Approval → Delivery → Service. Bottleneck: underwriting (14-45 days). Solutions: accelerated underwriting for healthy applicants, e-application, drop-ticket for simple cases. Target: 7-14 day cycle time.
